message:
feat(core,parser,render,layout): add class cardinality, namespace blocks, state notes/guards, and node icons

Class diagrams:
- Parse cardinality labels from quoted strings on relationships (e.g. `"1" --> "*"`)
  with new `strip_class_cardinality` and `ClassStatement::Cardinality` variant
- Support `namespace Name { ... }` blocks creating subgraphs/clusters for grouped classes

State diagrams:
- Parse single-line and multi-line `note right/left of State` into new `IrStateNote` struct
- Extract guard conditions (`[isValid]`) and actions (`/ cleanup()`) from transition labels
  into new `IrEdge.guard` and `IrEdge.action` fields

Node icons:
- Add `IrNode.icon` field and `IrBuilder::set_node_icon` for icon metadata
- Mindmap `::icon(...)` directives now populate `node.icon` (previously a no-op comment)
- Architecture service icons stored in `node.icon` alongside CSS classes
- Layout `compute_node_sizes` reserves vertical space for icon rendering
- SVG renderer draws inline icon shapes (server, database, cloud, lock, user, gear,
  mobile, desktop, container, queue, load-balancer, book) plus emoji fallback
- Icon CSS classes (`fm-node-icon-*`, `fm-node-has-icon`) emitted for styling hooks

Render:
- Cardinality labels rendered as small text near edge endpoints in class diagrams
- Node label text repositioned below icon when icon is present

Also: `FontMetricsDiagnostic` gains `Eq` derive, `avg_char_width` made `const fn`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3gq.1","title":"Implement advanced SVG visual effects: shadows, gradients, glow","description":"Add premium visual effects to SVG output that make diagrams look professional and polished:

1. DROP SHADOWS: Configurable SVG filter with feDropShadow. Soft shadow behind nodes and clusters. Adjustable: offset, blur radius, color, opacity. Default: subtle 2px offset, 4px blur, 20% opacity black.

2. GRADIENTS: Linear and radial gradient fills for nodes. Subtle top-to-bottom gradient gives depth. Configurable: gradient direction, start/end colors, midpoint.

3. GLOW EFFECT: Outer glow on selected/highlighted nodes. SVG filter with feGaussianBlur + feColorMatrix. Used for hover states and focus indicators.

4. ROUNDED EVERYTHING: Generous corner radii on all rectangular elements. Configurable globally and per-element. Default: 8px for nodes, 12px for clusters.

5. BORDER REFINEMENT: Thin, precise borders with antialiased rendering. Option for double borders on important nodes. Dashed borders for optional/external elements.

6. OPACITY LAYERS: Background clusters at 5-10% opacity for subtle grouping. Inactive/dimmed elements at 40% opacity for focus context.

All effects must be optional and configurable via theme. Performance: effects use SVG filters which are GPU-accelerated in browsers.

 {"id":"bd-3gq.2","title":"Implement advanced typography and text rendering","description":"Elevate text rendering quality in SVG diagrams:

1. FONT STACK: Default to modern system font stack (Inter, -apple-system, Segoe UI, etc.). Monospace stack for code labels (JetBrains Mono, Fira Code, etc.).

2. TEXT HIERARCHY: Node labels use medium weight, edge labels use regular weight, cluster titles use semibold. Configurable size scale.

3. TEXT FITTING: Intelligent label sizing that shrinks font size to fit within node bounds while maintaining minimum readable size (10px). Prefer ellipsis over tiny text.

4. MULTI-LINE LABELS: Proper text wrapping with SVG tspan elements. Break on word boundaries. Respect explicit newline characters in labels.

5. MARKDOWN IN LABELS: Support basic markdown in labels: bold, italic, code, strikethrough. Render as styled tspan elements.

6. TEXT MEASUREMENT: Approximate text width using character-count heuristic with font-specific correction factors. Allow calibration via config. This drives accurate node sizing.

7. LETTER SPACING: Subtle letter-spacing increase (0.01em) for labels to improve readability at small sizes.

 {"id":"bd-3gq.4","title":"Add CSS animation support for SVG diagrams","description":"Add optional CSS animations to SVG diagrams for enhanced visual communication:

1. ENTRANCE ANIMATIONS: Nodes and edges fade/slide in sequentially based on topological order. Creates a build-up effect showing the diagram structure progressively.

2. FLOW ANIMATIONS: Dashed edges with animated dash-offset to show data/control flow direction. Speed and dash pattern configurable.

3. PULSE ANIMATION: Gentle pulse on highlighted/active nodes. Used for drawing attention to specific elements.

4. HOVER EFFECTS: CSS :hover transitions on nodes (slight scale up, shadow increase, border color change). No JavaScript required.

5. TRANSITION SUPPORT: When diagram content changes, animate node position transitions (CSS transition on transform). Requires stable node IDs across renders.

All animations use CSS only (no SMIL, no JavaScript). Respect prefers-reduced-motion. Disabled by default, enabled via config flag.

 {"id":"bd-3gq.5","title":"Implement responsive SVG sizing and adaptive detail levels","description":"Make SVG diagrams look great at any size:

1. VIEWBOX-BASED SCALING: SVG uses viewBox with calculated bounds. Width/height set to 100% by default. Container determines rendered size. Preserve aspect ratio.

2. ADAPTIVE DETAIL: At small sizes (diagram area < threshold), automatically simplify: hide edge labels, truncate long node labels, reduce cluster label size. At tiny sizes: show only node shapes without text.

3. FONT SIZE FLOORS: Minimum readable font size (configurable, default 8px). Below this, switch to abbreviated labels or icons-only.

4. BREAKPOINT SYSTEM: Three detail tiers (Compact/Normal/Rich, matching FrankenTUI terminology). Auto-select based on available area. Manual override via config.

5. PRINT OPTIMIZATION: Special print media query styles embedded in SVG. Higher contrast, no animations, crisp borders, black text for readability.

 {"id":"bd-75ja","title":"Quotient Filters for Fast Duplicate Edge Detection","description":"## Quotient Filters for Fast Duplicate Edge Detection

Use quotient filters (a cache-friendly alternative to Bloom filters) to detect duplicate edges during graph construction, enabling O(1) amortized duplicate detection with minimal memory overhead.

## Mathematical Foundation
A quotient filter stores a set of fingerprints compactly using quotienting: for hash h(x), split into quotient q and remainder r where h(x) = q * 2^r_bits + r. Store remainder r in slot q of a compact array, using linear probing for collisions. Properties:
- Space: ~10-25% less than Bloom filters at the same false positive rate
- Cache behavior: elements stored in contiguous sorted runs → excellent locality
- False positive rate: 2^{-r_bits} (configurable via remainder size)
- No false negatives: if element was inserted, it will be found

**For duplicate edge detection:**
When parsing a Mermaid diagram, each edge (u, v) must be checked for duplicates. For n edges:
- Naive HashSet: O(n) space, O(1) per lookup, but HashSet overhead per entry
- Quotient filter: O(n * r_bits / 8) bytes, O(1) per lookup, much lower constant factor
- For r_bits = 16: false positive rate = 1/65536, space = 2 bytes per edge
- For 10000 edges: quotient filter = ~20KB vs HashSet = ~400KB (20x savings)

**Practical consideration:** The space savings are significant for WASM target where memory is constrained. For native target, the HashSet overhead is negligible. This is primarily a WASM optimization.

## Key Papers
- Bender et al., "Don't Thrash: How to Cache Your Hash on Flash" (VLDB 2012) — quotient filter
- Pandey et al., "A General-Purpose Counting Filter" (SIGMOD 2017) — counting quotient filter
- Fan et al., "Cuckoo Filter: Practically Better Than Bloom" (CoNEXT 2014) — alternative approach

## Implementation in FrankenMermaid
1. Implement `QuotientFilter<H>` generic over hash function.
2. Use FxHash for fingerprint computation (fast, non-cryptographic).
3. Implement `insert(edge_hash)` and `may_contain(edge_hash)` operations.
4. On `may_contain() == true`: verify with exact edge comparison (handle false positives).
5. Replace `HashSet<(NodeId, NodeId)>` in parser with `QuotientFilter` for WASM target.
6. Keep `HashSet` for native target (simpler, negligible space overhead).
7. Benchmark: memory usage and parse time for 1000, 10000, 100000 edge graphs on WASM.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] QuotientFilter implementation with configurable false positive rate
- [ ] Duplicate edge detection correct (no false negatives, false positives handled)
- [ ] Memory reduction >= 10x vs HashSet for 10000-edge graphs
- [ ] Parse time comparable to HashSet (within 10%)
- [ ] WASM-specific optimization (cfg-gated)
- [ ] Property test: no false negatives over 100000 random edge sets
- [ ] Decision contract: adopt for WASM if memory reduction > 5x","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:46:30.205093497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:09.655567581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-cs","parser","probabilistic","quotient-filter"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-75ja","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:09.655486068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-75ja","depends_on_id":"bd-3bc.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:52.362224276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-77gh","title":"Implement IrStyleDef and IrInlineStyle types in fm-core for styling support","description":"# Styling Infrastructure in fm-core

## Purpose
Multiple diagram types need styling support (classDef, style, linkStyle directives). This task creates the shared IR types that all styling features depend on.

## Types to Add
1. IrStyleDef { name: String, properties: BTreeMap<String, String> } — for classDef definitions
2. IrInlineStyle(BTreeMap<String, String>) — for style/linkStyle directives
3. Add style_defs: Vec<IrStyleDef> to MermaidDiagramIr
4. Add inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle> to IrNode
5. Add inline_style: Option<IrInlineStyle> to IrEdge

## SVG Property Mapping
Document the mapping from CSS-like property names to SVG attributes:
- fill → rect/path fill attribute
- stroke → rect/path stroke attribute
- stroke-width → stroke-width attribute
- stroke-dasharray → stroke-dasharray
- color → text fill attribute
- font-size → font-size
- font-weight → font-weight

## Security

 {"id":"bd-7g6d","title":"Parse and render sequence diagram notes","description":"# Sequence Diagram Notes

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
sequenceDiagram
  Note left of Alice: Single line note
  Note right of Bob: Another note
  Note over Alice,Bob: Spanning note
  Note over Alice: Multi-line<br/>note text
\`\`\`
- \`Note left of Participant:\` — note box to the left of lifeline
- \`Note right of Participant:\` — note box to the right of lifeline
- \`Note over Participant:\` — note box centered over lifeline
- \`Note over P1,P2:\` — note spanning between two participants
- Notes have a distinctive folded-corner box shape

## Current State
- NOT parsed. Note lines produce "unsupported sequence syntax" warnings.
- No SequenceStatement variant for notes.

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Add SequenceStatement::Note { position: NotePosition, participants: Vec<String>, text: String }. Parse \`Note left of\`, \`Note right of\`, \`Note over\` with participant name(s) and colon-delimited text.
2. **IR**: Store notes in a Vec<IrSequenceNote> on MermaidDiagramIr or in an IrSequenceMeta struct.
3. **Layout**: Notes occupy vertical space like messages. Position note boxes at the appropriate x (left/right/over participant) and y (between messages).
4. **SVG Renderer**: Draw note boxes with folded corner (small triangle in top-right). Add text with word wrapping.
5. **Tests**: Left note, right note, over single, over span, multi-line.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:44:09.583994264Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-18T02:06:14.640336586Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T02:06:14.639876235Z","close_reason":"Implemented sequence diagram note parsing.  {"id":"bd-qiyv","title":"Make quadrant chart dimensions configurable","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded quadrant chart dimensions (chart_w=400, chart_h=400, margin_left=80, margin_top=60) with configurable or content-adaptive values.

## Why
Both the layout (layout_diagram_quadrant_traced) and SVG renderer (render_quadrant_svg) use identical hardcoded 400x400 chart dimensions. This means:
- The chart does not adapt to the available viewport/terminal size
- Long axis labels can overflow the margins
- The chart is always square even if the content suggests a different aspect ratio
- Terminal rendering at small sizes wastes most of the 400x400 area

## Proposed Approach
1. Add `chart_width` and `chart_height` fields to a `QuadrantLayoutConfig` (or reuse LayoutSpacing)
2. Compute margins from axis label dimensions using FontMetrics
3. In terminal renderer, scale to the actual cell dimensions instead of using fixed 400x400
4. Keep 400x400 as the default for backward compatibility

## Acceptance Criteria
- Quadrant charts render correctly at different terminal sizes
- Long axis labels do not overflow
- Default behavior unchanged for typical usage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:10.923449900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:39:08.407968687Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:39:08.407452109Z","close_reason":"Quadrant chart_w/chart_h now scale with point count: base=300+15*n, clamped [200,600]. Margin_left computed from axis label width. Square aspect preserved.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-sb52","title":"Make sequence note width content-adaptive","description":"## What
Replace the hardcoded `note_width = 120.0` in build_sequence_note_geometry() with a value computed from the note text content using FontMetrics.

## Why
The sequence diagram layout uses a fixed 120px width for ALL notes regardless of content. This means:
- Short notes ("OK") waste space with a 120px box
- Long notes ("This is a detailed explanation of the process flow") truncate or overflow
- Multi-participant "Over" notes that span wide participants also use the same 120px base

## Proposed Approach
1. Compute note width from `FontMetrics::estimate_dimensions(note.text)` + padding
2. Clamp to a reasonable range: `min(80.0, max(text_width + 24.0, 200.0))`
3. For "Over" notes spanning multiple participants, use `max(computed_width, participant_span + padding)`
4. Similarly, `note_line_height = 16.0` and `note_vertical_padding = 12.0` should derive from font metrics

## Dependencies
This requires FontMetrics to be passed to build_sequence_note_geometry(). Currently the function receives `message_y_positions`, `participant_x_centers`, and `node_sizes` but NOT FontMetrics. The signature needs extending.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Short notes render compact
- Long notes expand to fit text
- Multi-line notes properly sized for all lines
- "Over" notes spanning participants are at least as wide as the span","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:26.384865805Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:40:51.477852877Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:40:51.477341249Z","close_reason":"Note width now computed from content: widest_line * avg_char_w + 24, clamped [80, 300].  {"id":"bd-u4c1","title":"Replace hardcoded SVG band/axis colors with CSS custom properties","description":"## What
Replace hardcoded hex color strings in `render_layout_band()` and `render_layout_axis_tick()` functions in fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs with CSS custom property references.

## Why
These functions render gantt section labels (#4a5568), axis tick marks (#94a3b8), and axis tick labels (#64748b). These colors are theme-unaware — they are legible on the Default light theme but invisible on Dark, Neon, and Blueprint themes where the background is dark.

## Technical Context
These two functions (`render_layout_band` at line ~2050 and `render_layout_axis_tick` at line ~2104) only receive `&SvgRenderConfig` — they do NOT have access to the `Theme` struct. The options are:
1. Thread `&ThemeColors` through the function signatures (invasive refactor)
2. Use CSS custom properties like `var(--fm-text-color, #fallback)` (minimally invasive)

Option 2 is preferred because the theme CSS already defines these properties via `ThemeColors::to_css_vars()` in theme.rs, and SVGs with embedded `<style>` blocks will correctly resolve them.

## Specific Changes
- `render_layout_band()` line ~2095: Change `.fill("#4a5568")` to `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #4a5568)")`
- `render_layout_axis_tick()` line ~2112: Change `.stroke("#94a3b8")` to `.stroke("var(--fm-edge-color, #94a3b8)")`
- `render_layout_axis_tick()` line ~2124: Change `.fill("#64748b")` to `.fill("var(--fm-text-color, #64748b)")`

## Status
PARTIALLY DONE — The CSS var approach was applied in the latest session. Verify the changes are committed and golden tests blessed.

- Axis ticks use `var(--fm-edge-color)` for stroke and `var(--fm-text-color)` for text

 {"id":"bd-u785","title":"EPIC: Killer Demo — Layout Regression Test Harness","description":"## Killer Demo — Layout Regression Test Harness

Build an automated visual regression testing harness that renders the complete FrankenMermaid test corpus to SVG/PNG, compares against golden reference images using perceptual diff, and produces a browsable HTML report. This serves dual purpose: regression detection AND an impressive demo of FrankenMermaid's rendering capabilities.

## Motivation
Layout algorithms are notoriously difficult to regression-test with unit tests alone. A pixel-level change in coordinate assignment can propagate to produce visually different (but structurally correct) layouts. Conversely, a bug might produce structurally incorrect output that looks "close enough" to pass manual review. The regression harness provides:
1. **Automated comparison:** perceptual diff between current and golden renders
2. **Browsable report:** HTML gallery showing before/after/diff for each test case
3. **Demo showcase:** the report itself IS the demo — browse all diagram types, see FrankenMermaid's quality
4. **Regression budget:** configure acceptable diff threshold (e.g., 0.1% pixel difference) per diagram type

## Perceptual Diff Algorithm
Use structural similarity index (SSIM) or perceptual hash (pHash) rather than pixel-exact comparison:
- **SSIM:** Compares luminance, contrast, and structure. Score from 0 to 1, where 1 = identical.
  SSIM(x,y) = [l(x,y)]^α * [c(x,y)]^β * [s(x,y)]^γ
  where l = luminance, c = contrast, s = structure comparison
- **pHash:** DCT-based perceptual hash. Hamming distance between hashes measures visual similarity.
- **Threshold:** SSIM > 0.99 = no regression, 0.95-0.99 = warning, < 0.95 = regression

## Implementation
1. Create `regression-harness/` binary crate in workspace.
2. Input: directory of .mmd files (test corpus) + golden reference renders.
3. Pipeline per file: parse → layout → render to SVG → rasterize to PNG (via resvg or similar).
4. Compare each PNG against golden reference using SSIM.
5. Generate HTML report: thumbnail grid, click to expand, before/after/diff overlay.
6. Golden reference update: `cargo run --bin regression-harness -- --update-goldens`.
7. CI integration: run on every PR that touches fm-layout or fm-render, fail if any SSIM < threshold.
8. Include timing data per render in the report (performance regression detection).

## Demo Aspect
The regression report doubles as the project's visual showcase:
- Categories: flowcharts, sequence diagrams, class diagrams, state diagrams, gantt charts, etc.
- Each category shows 5-10 representative diagrams at various complexities.
- Report includes metadata: node count, edge count, layout time, render time.
- Hosted version can be published to frankenmermaid.com/gallery for public demo.

## Success Metrics
- All diagram types in test corpus rendered and compared automatically
- SSIM threshold tuned to catch real regressions while ignoring sub-pixel jitter
- HTML report loads in < 2 seconds with 500+ thumbnails
- Golden update workflow is one command + explicit commit
- CI gate blocks merge on regression (SSIM < 0.95)
- Report generation completes in < 5 minutes for full corpus

## Dependencies
- Requires fm-render-svg (bd-1y5) for SVG output
- Requires test corpus (bd-2xl.1 golden test infrastructure)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:39.374613334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:18.947294342Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","layout","regression","testing","visual"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-17e4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:30:18.947218691Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-1y5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:44.324472620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u785","depends_on_id":"bd-2xl.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:45:44.427880609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
